actionsTaken: Added Tier 1 schema-contract signal tests at tests/scripts/signal-tests/tier-1-schema-contracts.test.js. 9 probes across 3 handoffs: H1 indexer→manifest-generator (3 probes — required field surface, consumer-reads-only-producer-fields, end-to-end shape), H2 parser-persistence→graph-persister (3 probes — node_type enum coverage of parser entity kinds, ProjectFiles INSERT column contract, edge_type enum coverage), H3 St8SchemaCard ↔ manifest-generator load-bearing omissions (3 probes — canonical shape carries lifecyclePhase + birthTimestamp, projection omits both, fingerprint round-trip preserves birthTimestamp). Mutation probe verified: renaming `fingerprint:` key to `fp_test:` in manifest-generator caused P1.3 to fail; restore returned to 9/9 pass.

userNote: Orphan-detection (check #6) over-reports for frontend files loaded via <script src> from src/frontend/index.html. Today the script skips src/frontend/ entirely (line 336) so this is latent — but if anyone ever adds a <script src='src/features/...'> include directly to the HTML shell, it will be reported as orphan. Make the skip-list configurable, or scan index.html for <script src> tags and treat those as 'referenced'.
actionsTaken: Added collectHtmlScriptRefs() that parses src/frontend/index.html (and any future HTML shells) for <script src='...'> tags and resolves them to absolute paths, seeding the 'referenced' set in checkOrphans. Removed the blanket src/frontend/ skip — now a frontend file is only suppressed if it's actually referenced (from HTML or from a require()), or it's in the expectedOrphans allowlist, or it lives under src/frontend/vendor/. Exported collectHtmlScriptRefs + checkOrphans for unit tests and gated main() behind require.main === module. Added tests/scripts/migration/check-conventions.test.js (5 cases): script-tag parsing, CDN/absolute-URL skip, missing-file no-throw, ref-vs-non-ref classification, and an integration check that real components in index.html aren't orphans. Verified: `node check-conventions.js` reports 11 orphans (the known-residual non-frontend cases), DOWN from 16 — the 5 file-explorer/terminal/graph-viewer/settings/coordination files previously masked by the blanket skip are now correctly seen as referenced.
reviewNote: Verified: read collectHtmlScriptRefs() + checkOrphans(), confirmed HTML-aware reference seeding replaces blanket src/frontend/ skip. Ran `node scripts/migration/check-conventions.js` — Orphan modules: 11 (matches claim, down from 16). Targeted tests pass 5/5. main() correctly gated by require.main === module. Tests 398/398.

userNote: Browser-only detection relies on the manifest's `client: true` flag (line 163). If someone forgets to set it on a frontend file move, verify.js tries to require() the module, which throws on window/document references. The batch 017 both-broken-identically check (line 176-186) mitigates this to a WARN-pass — but it's noisy and conceals real regressions in the same file. Consider auto-detecting client modules by path prefix (anything under src/frontend/) instead of trusting the flag.
actionsTaken: Added detectBrowserOnly(absPath) parse heuristic that strips block + line comments and scans for direct member access against browser globals: window, document, navigator, location, localStorage, sessionStorage, history.{pushState,replaceState,back,forward}, customElements, HTMLElement, plus `typeof window|document|navigator` guards. Anchored each pattern on a non-word char so lookalikes like myWindow/documentation don't trigger. Wired into the isClient decision: `m.client === true || heuristicBrowser`. Emits a hint line when manifest.client is missing but the heuristic fires, so reviewers see the auto-detection in the log. Exported detectBrowserOnly for tests, guarded main() with require.main === module. Added tests/scripts/migration/verify.test.js (12 cases) covering each pattern, comment-aware negatives, lookalike negatives, missing-file no-throw, plus integration probes against src/frontend/app.js (positive) and src/core/hook-registry.js (negative). Known limitation documented inline: SQL string literals containing documentary 'window.X' references will false-positive — harmless because verify.js then runs `node --check` instead of require() (downgrade, never upgrade).
reviewNote: Verified: detectBrowserOnly() contains 10 patterns (window, document, navigator, location, localStorage, sessionStorage, history.pushState/etc, typeof window/document/navigator, customElements, HTMLElement) with non-word-char anchoring. Comment-stripping (block then line) runs BEFORE pattern match. Targeted tests pass 12/12. Mutation probe: removed the window pattern -> "detects window.X member access as browser-only" failed as expected; restored cleanly. Tests 398/398.

userNote: Batch 019 documents OGB cleanup as 'founder-driven destruction' but provides no automated tooling. After founder destroys OGB/ contents, empty subdirectories remain (OGB/, OGB/backend/, OGB/lib/utils/, OGB/lib/commands/, OGB/lib/commands/integr8/, OGB/src/frontend/services/). Need a cleanup-empty-dirs helper that walks bottom-up and removes empty dirs.
actionsTaken: Built scripts/migration/ogb-destroy.js with companion tests at tests/scripts/migration/ogb-destroy.test.js (6 probes). Tool flow: (1) inventory OGB/ files+dirs, (2) Invariant A — grep src/, tests/, scripts/, start.js, package.json for any live require/import into OGB/ (excludes own file and pure comment lines); aborts exit 1 with hits if any found, (3) plan preview in --dry-run default mode, (4) under --yes, writes timestamped tar.gz backup to .st8/ogb-backup/ before any rm, then removes files, then bottom-up rmdirs the now-empty subdirectories, then OGB/ itself. Founder still pulls the trigger via --yes; this script provides the safety net.

userNote: manifest.json is OVERWRITTEN each batch — there's no audit trail of what each batch's manifest contained (description, generatedAt, the specific move list at decision-time). move-history.json captures the successful from/to pairs but loses the description + per-batch metadata. Mitigation: archive each batch's manifest.json under scripts/migration/manifest-history/<batch-name>.json on successful verify.
actionsTaken: Implemented variant (a) from the wave-6b prompt: append-only manifest-history.jsonl that captures one self-contained JSON record per batch — batch, description, generatedAt, verifiedAt (write-time ISO), gitCommit (short SHA), moves[], plus optional notes and followUps. Wired appendManifestHistory() into verify.js's success path (fail === 0) so each clean batch verification writes its own audit record. Idempotency key is (batch name, moves signature) — re-running verify after later commits land cannot duplicate a batch's record. Added scripts/migration/backfill-manifest-history.js which walks `git log --diff-filter=AM scripts/migration/manifest.json --reverse`, runs `git show <sha>:scripts/migration/manifest.json` at each commit, parses, and appends. Source for backfill: all 12 batch commits in the master tree (ab4d038 shared → 9cca4b8 background-indexer). Entries added to history: 12, covering 46 file moves across shared, core-database, lifecycle-watcher, schema-cards, prd, analysis, integr8-core, indexing-parsers, indexing-engine, server-and-entry, frontend-components, background-indexer. Added tests/scripts/migration/manifest-history.test.js (8 cases): line/JSON shape, idempotency (same commit AND later commit), distinct-batches, optional fields, missing-file, malformed-line tolerance, plus an integration check that the real repo backfill contains all 12 expected batches.
reviewNote: Verified: scripts/migration/manifest-history.jsonl has exactly 12 entries (one per batch). Inspected entries shared/analysis/background-indexer — each carries batch, description, generatedAt, verifiedAt, gitCommit, moves[]. Idempotency: re-ran backfill-manifest-history.js -> "Done: 0 appended, 12 skipped." Line count stays 12. Targeted tests pass 8/8. Tests 398/398.

userNote: Given a current path like 'src/features/indexing/background-indexer.js' or an OGB path 'OGB/lib/commands/typeParser.js.txt', there's no reverse-lookup tool to find which batch moved it. Today requires manually grepping the moves arrays in move-history.json. A small CLI: `node scripts/migration/which-batch.js <path>` would scan completedBatches and return the batch name + position.

FK-cascade gotcha worth documenting: file_registry is FK-referenced by connections, file_intent, file_mutation_log. The pruner (lines 285-294 and 319-327) correctly operates per-fingerprint inside a transaction, deleting children BEFORE the registry row. Don't try to bulk-DELETE from file_registry via filepath — you'll get FK violations because children reference the about-to-be-deleted rows. Add a guard comment at the top of persistence.js explicitly stating 'all file_registry deletes must go through delete*ForFile(fingerprint)' patterns.

No versioned migration system for the SQLite schema. All schema is currently inlined as CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS statements in persistence.js initialize() / migration. If the schema ever needs to evolve (e.g. add a column, change a constraint, drop a deprecated field), there's no infrastructure for ordered migrations. Need a scripts/migrations/<timestamp>-<name>.sql convention + a runner.

Per bible batch 021, the cumulative gap-check tally still shows 31 findings (down from 282): 25 expected boundary violations (core/server orchestrating features — correct architecture) + 6 orphan candidates needing human review (ground-plane.js, integr8/index.js, migration-executor.js, background-indexer.js, graph/builder.js, graph/traversal.js). Some of those 6 are CLI entry points (intentional orphans); some are dormant pending sonic restoration. Need a documented allowlist in check-conventions.js so the report cleanly distinguishes 'expected orphan' from 'unexpected orphan'.

Dormant file — throws Cannot find module './sonicClient.js' at module load. Three references to absent siblings (sonicClient.js, multiPassAnalyzer.js, precisionCapture.js) were stripped during the pre-refactor stubs-cleanup. The file is currently a 812-line orphan kept around for future sonic restoration. Bible batch 027 (sonic-foundation) appears to be addressing this — verify whether sonic restoration is complete and whether background-indexer.js is now wired or still dormant.
